channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This free web-based image editor gives Photoshop a run for its money
item.description: AI image editing may be all the rage, but good old-fashioned image editors are still essential. There’s a problem, though: Windows PCs, Chromebooks, and Macs don’t include exceptional image editors that go beyond the most basic editing needs.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: M4 MacBook Air 2025 review: The perfect laptop for most consumers
item.description: Apple’s new MacBook Air laptops, which the company unveiled alongside a new iPad Air and a disappointing 11th-generation iPad earlier this month, are now on sale. They feature the M4 chip and some additional upgrades that remote workers and desk jockeys, in particular, may appreciate.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: FBI warns of dangerous new ‘smishing' scam targeting your phone
item.description: Hackers have set up over 10,000 fake websites to keep smishing scams going and target both iPhone and Android users with texts. Kurt the CyberGuy shares how to defend yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI dashcams enhance trucker safety while raising privacy concerns
item.description: Dashcams that use artificial intelligence improve trucking safety and efficiency, but they also raise privacy concerns for drivers and pedestrians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 400 million unwanted and malicious emails were received by businesses in 2024
item.description: Report highlights rising cyber threats, and urges businesses to adopt advanced cybersecurity practices and foster a strong security culture.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX launches a new crew to the space station to replace NASA's stuck astronauts
item.description: The replacements for NASA's two stuck astronauts are on their way to the International Space Station.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Influencer Brandon Edelman made $768,000 in a year—and what he actually took home
item.description: We all know influencing can pay well—but just how well? Philadelphia-based influencer Brandon Edelman, known online as @bran_flakezz, recently went viral on TikTok after revealing he made $768,000 last year, primarily from brand partnerships and creator funds.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: JDK 25: The new features in Java 25
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 25, a planned long-term support release of standard Java due in September, now has two features officially proposed for it. The first is a preview of an API for stable values, a feature that promises to speed up the startup of Java applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple Original Films will take you behind-the-scenes of a racing cockpit in this new thrilling F1 movie trailer
item.description: The trailer for the new Apple TV+ Original movie F1 shows off Brad Pitt and Damson Idris in a racing comeback story.
item.dc:creator: amelia.schwanke@futurenet.com (Amelia Schwanke), TechRadar

item.title: For March’s Patch Tuesday, 57 fixes
item.description: For so few patches from Microsoft this month (57), we have seven zero-days to manage (with a “Patch Now” recommendation for Windows) and standard release schedules for Microsoft Office, Microsoft browsers (Edge) and Visual Studio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why DeepSeek’s arrival looks like a key moment for safe AI
item.description: The AI industry is growing up fast. New model releases are now a regular event and premium AI features are quickly overtaken by free or freemium alternatives.
item.dc:creator: Donnchadh Casey, Fast Company

item.title: Postponed NASA, Space X Falcon 9 mission rescheduled for Friday
item.description: NASA and SpaceX rescheduled the launch of their Falcon 9 rocket rescue mission to the International Space Station for Friday. They were unable to launch on Wednesday due to issues with equipment on the ground, and opted to scrub a Thursday launch attempt due to weather.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: DOGE may be using an algorithm to fire federal workers
item.description: In the past month and a half, the Trump Administration has drastically reduced the federal government workforce. The cuts alone have generated concern and anger among workers and those who rely on US government services.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Mozilla: Update Firefox immediately
item.description: Expired certificates have recently caused a lot of chaos, including for Chromecast users. With that in mind, Mozilla is now urging all Firefox users to immediately update the browser to the latest version.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Greece says ambitious Mediterranean power cable project is on track despite setbacks
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — The future of an ambitious electricity cable linking the power grids of Greece, Cyprus and Israel by the end of the decade is on track despite a series of setbacks, Greece’s Foreign Minister Giorgos Gerapetritis said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: OpenAI calls for US to centralize AI regulation
item.description: OpenAI executives think the federal government should regulate artificial intelligence in the US, taking precedence over often more restrictive state regulations.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Dangerous Chrome extensions mimic password managers
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy explains how to protect yourself from malicious Chrome extensions that can steal sensitive information like password managers, crypto wallets and banking apps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: Facebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18. It will initially based on a ratings system used by Elon Musk's X. Meta ended its fact-checking program in January.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google reportedly looking at smart glasses
item.description: After abandoning its Google Glass smart glasses in 2023, Google seems ready to take another kick at the can with what will reportedly be a $115 million acquisition of Canadian startup AdHawk Microsystems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple Faces Renewed Threat in UK Over Mobile Browser Market
item.description: UK's top antitrust regulator says Apple and Google are holding back innovation in the mobile browser market, with most criticism leveled at Apple.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5
